hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: 32.7% gives Arizona just under 1-in-3 odds to win it all, pricing them as a legitimate tournament threat but well below blue-chip status, which tracks if you believe their talent ceiling matters more than their inconsistency. The +2.2 point spike on $1.7M volume suggests sharp money is loading up on their tournament upside, but you need to know whether this is genuine Wildcats improvement or just typical mid-season overreaction to a few good wins.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's Tournament Odds Jump 2.2pts. Here's Why the Sharp Money Keeps Coming.
daily_take.deck: Arizona popped 2.2 percentage points overnight to 32.7%, now sitting as a legitimate Final Four contender on Polymarket's radar. The move signals real conviction from the smart money, not noise. At 32.7%, bettors are pricing in elite recruiting, a clean NCAA slate, and consistent tournament performance. The question isn't whether Arizona can win it, it's whether the market has finally caught up to what college basketball insiders already knew. This is a market finding equilibrium, not a misprice begging to be faded.

sidebar.headline: McIlroy's Masters odds ticked up 1.5pts to 6.5%, a whisper of a move that reflects his consistency metrics. Still massively undervalued against Tiger-adjacent narrative power.
sidebar.label: McIlroy Masters 2026: 6.5%
